name: Undo
problem: Users need a fast way to recover from an action without being blocked before every action.
solution: Allow the action to complete, communicate the result, and provide a time-bound or persistent way to reverse it.
problemContext: A frequent action can be performed quickly and mistakes are plausible.
The system can reliably restore the prior state after the action.
selectionRules: Choose undo when recovery is reliable and the action is frequent enough that confirmation would slow users down.
Prefer confirmation when the action has irreversible external side effects.
Use persistent recovery, not a short toast, when the consequence is important or recovery needs time.
requiredStates: Action-completed state that clearly communicates what changed.
Recoverable state with an undo or restore affordance.
Final state after undo expires or is completed.
interactionContract: The undo affordance must be reachable by keyboard and visible long enough to use.
Undo must restore the relevant object, selection, or previous value.
The system must communicate whether undo succeeded or is no longer available.
implementationChecklist: Expose a clearly labeled undo or restore control.
Preserve enough state to accurately reverse the action.
Avoid moving focus unexpectedly after routine actions.
commonMisuses: Offering undo for an action that cannot actually be reversed.
Hiding the undo action in a transient message that disappears too quickly.
Using undo without confirming that the restored state is accurate.
critiqueQuestions: Is recovery reliable enough that the user can act first and fix mistakes afterward?
useWhen: The action is common and mistakes are likely.
The system can reliably restore the prior state.
avoidWhen: The action has external side effects that cannot be reversed.
The user needs to understand a serious consequence before the action happens.
accessibility: The undo control must be keyboard reachable.
The recovery option should not disappear before assistive technology users can perceive it.
keyboardBehavior: Keyboard shortcuts can supplement visible undo controls.
Focus should not be unexpectedly stolen after routine actions.
failureModes: Showing an undo action too briefly.
Pretending an irreversible action can be undone.
Relying on a toast-only recovery path for critical changes.

sources.note: Alert guidance explicitly contrasts undoable destructive actions with cases requiring alerts.
